```{r}
#| warning: false
#| code-fold: true
#| include: false

subfoldername <- "../../outputs"

# inline_code_list: turn a vector (column names, factor levels, file names) into one readable,
# backticked, comma-separated inline phrase with an Oxford "and". Use in prose to replace raw
# colnames()/unique()/head() console dumps, e.g. Columns in `dat`: `r inline_code_list(colnames(dat))`.
inline_code_list <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x); x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return("")
  x <- paste0("`", x, "`")
  if (length(x) == 1) return(x)
  if (length(x) == 2) return(paste(x, collapse = " and "))
  paste0(paste(x[-length(x)], collapse = ", "), ", and ", x[length(x)])
}

# make data file name
mkfilefn <- function(datname, sf = subfoldername){
  filename <- 
  paste(section,
        deparse(substitute(datname)),
  paste(length(unique(datname$site)),"sites",sep=""),
  minyear,
  maxyear,
  sep="_")
  filename <- paste(sf,"/",
                    filename,".csv",sep="")
  return(filename)
}

#make metadata file name
mkmetafilefn <- function(datname, sf = subfoldername){
  filename <- 
  paste(section,
        deparse(substitute(datname)),
  paste(length(unique(datname$site)),"sites",sep=""),
  minyear,
  maxyear,
  sep="_")
  filename <- paste(sf,"/",
                    filename,".txt",sep="")
  return(filename)
}

#summarize the data frame
summarize_data_frame <- function(df, dfdesc, desc) {
  summary_text <- character(0)
  
  # Adding a decorative line of "#" characters at the top
  summary_text <- c(summary_text, "###############################")
  
  filename <-   
    paste(section,
          deparse(substitute(df)),
          paste(length(unique(df$site)),"sites",sep=""),
          minyear,
          maxyear,
          sep="_")
  filename <- paste(filename,".csv",sep="")
  
  # Adding details to summary
  summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("filename:", filename),
                    # paste("generated by:", " Lauren Olinger (LO) "),
                    paste("date:", Sys.time()),
                    paste("description:", dfdesc),
                    "###############################", # Adding the line of "#" characters
                    "", # Adding an extra empty line
                    "column names:", paste(names(df), collapse = " "))
  
  # Iterating through each column to summarize
  for(column_name in names(df)) {
    summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("\ncolumn:", column_name),
                      paste("description:", desc$description[which(desc$column_names == column_name)]),
                      paste("Data Type:", class(df[[column_name]])))
    
    # Checking if the column is numeric
    if (is.numeric(df[[column_name]])) {
      summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("Mean:", mean(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE)),
                        paste("Min:", min(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE)),
                        paste("Max:", max(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE)))
    } else {
      # If the column is non-numeric, list the unique values
      unique_values <- unique(df[[column_name]])
      summary_text <- c(summary_text, "Unique Values:")
      for (value in unique_values) {
        # Adding each unique value on a separate line, indented
        summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("  ", value))
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(summary_text)
}


make_desc <- function(df){
  desc <- data.frame(column_names = names(df), 
                                 description = "insert here")
  # cat("now define desc$description: \n")
  # cat("desc$description <- c(x,\n
  #                            y,\n
  #                            z)\n")
  return(desc)
}

# function `getSiteData`: makes a new dataframe `dat_siteinfo` with same number of rows as `dat` that stores the site data for each row of `dat` , no matter what format that is in. at this stage, helps remove unwanted sites based on criteria like yearadded. arguments `dat` - data frame want to make equivalent site data for arguments `colindOfSite` - the column index of the site name in `dat` that will be xreffed and matched to `sitedat`. for example in below exaple, the site column is stored in 4th column in `tcrmp_benthicDat`. For now only pulling out location, yearadded, depth, but in future can add more, such as gps coords, rugosity, for plotting, but this could eat up memory for large `dats`.
getSiteData <- function(dat, colindOfSite) {
  # validation (Phase D): the apply() below silently drops rows whose site is absent from the
  # site master (which() returns integer(0)), which misaligns dat_siteinfo against dat. Warn so
  # the mismatch is visible rather than silent.
  .missing <- setdiff(unique(dat[[colindOfSite]]), sitedat$site)
  if (length(.missing) > 0)
    warning("getSiteData: ", length(.missing), " site(s) not in the site master (rows dropped): ",
            paste(.missing, collapse = ", "))
  row_numbers <- unlist(apply(dat, 1, function(row) {
    matching_element <- row[colindOfSite]
    which(sitedat$site == matching_element)
  }))
  
  dat_siteinfo <- data.frame(
    Location = sitedat$site[row_numbers],
    yearadded = sitedat$yearadded[row_numbers],
    depth = sitedat$depth[row_numbers]
  )
  return(dat_siteinfo)
}

# Single function to process and save data, including generating filenames, writing CSVs, and creating metadata

save_csv_and_metadata <- function(input_df, df_desc, column_descriptions, filename, wheretosave = getwd(), minyear = NULL, maxyear = NULL, site_column = NULL, time = FALSE) {
  
  timenow <- Sys.time()  # Capture current time once for consistency
  
  format_time <- function(time) {
    # Format system time for filenames
    format(time, "%b_%d_%Y_%X") %>%
      gsub(" ", "_", .) %>%
      gsub(":", "_", .) %>%
      gsub("-", "_", .)
  }
  
  mkfilefn <- function() {
    # Build the filename based on inputs
    name <- filename
    if (!is.null(site_column)) name <- paste(name, paste(length(unique(input_df[[site_column]])), "sites", sep = ""), sep = "_")
    if (!is.null(minyear) && !is.null(maxyear)) name <- paste(name, minyear, maxyear, sep = "_")
    if (time) name <- paste(name, format_time(timenow), sep = "_")
    paste(wheretosave, "/", name, ".csv", sep = "")
  }
  
  mkmetafilefn <- function() {
    # Build the metadata filename
    name <- filename
    if (!is.null(site_column)) name <- paste(name, paste(length(unique(input_df[[site_column]])), "sites", sep = ""), sep = "_")
    if (!is.null(minyear) && !is.null(maxyear)) name <- paste(name, minyear, maxyear, sep = "_")
    if (time) name <- paste(name, format_time(timenow), sep = "_")
    paste(wheretosave, "/", name, ".txt", sep = "")
  }
  
  summarize_data_frame <- function(df, dfdesc, desc) {
    # Summarize dataframe columns, including unique values or ranges for dates and numerics
    summary_text <- c("###############################")
    name <- filename
    if (!is.null(site_column)) name <- paste(name, paste(length(unique(df[[site_column]])), "sites", sep = ""), sep = "_")
    if (!is.null(minyear) && !is.null(maxyear)) name <- paste(name, minyear, maxyear, sep = "_")
    if (time) name <- paste(name, format_time(timenow), sep = "_")
    name <- paste(name, ".csv", sep = "")
    
    summary_text <- c(
      summary_text, paste("filename:", name), paste("date:", Sys.time()),
      paste("description:", dfdesc), "###############################", "",
      "column names:", paste(names(df), collapse = " ")
    )
    
    for (column_name in names(df)) {
      summary_text <- c(
        summary_text, paste("\ncolumn:", column_name),
        paste("description:", desc$description[which(desc$column_names == column_name)]),
        paste("Data Type:", class(df[[column_name]]))
      )
      
      if (is.numeric(df[[column_name]])) {
        summary_text <- c(
          summary_text, paste("Mean:", mean(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE)),
          paste("Min:", min(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE)),
          paste("Max:", max(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE))
        )
      } else if (inherits(df[[column_name]], "Date")) {
        summary_text <- c(
          summary_text, paste("Date Range:", min(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE), "to", max(df[[column_name]], na.rm = TRUE))
        )
      } else {
        unique_values <- unique(df[[column_name]])
        n_unique <- length(unique_values)
        value_counts <- table(df[[column_name]])
        has_duplicates <- any(value_counts > 1)
        
        if (n_unique > 100) {
          if (has_duplicates) {
            freq_values <- names(sort(value_counts, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10])
            summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("Unique Values: (showing 10 most frequent of", n_unique, "entries)"))
            for (value in freq_values) summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("  ", value))
          } else {
            set.seed(42)  # reproducible example sampling (Phase D): stable .txt across renders
            example_values <- sample(unique_values, 10)
            summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("Unique Values: (showing 10 examples of", n_unique, "entries)"))
            for (value in example_values) summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("  ", value))
          }
        } else {
          summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("Unique Values: (showing all", n_unique, "entries)"))
          for (value in unique_values) summary_text <- c(summary_text, paste("  ", value))
        }
      }
    }
    
    summary_text
  }
  
  make_desc <- function(df) {
    # Create a skeleton for the column descriptions
    data.frame(column_names = names(df), description = "insert here")
  }
  
  # Save the dataframe as CSV
  csv_filename <- mkfilefn()
  write.csv(input_df, csv_filename, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Create and save the metadata file
  input_df_desc_full <- make_desc(input_df)
  input_df_desc_full$description <- column_descriptions
  summary_text <- summarize_data_frame(input_df, df_desc, input_df_desc_full)
  
  metadata_filename <- mkmetafilefn()
  file_conn <- file(metadata_filename, "w")
  writeLines(summary_text, file_conn)
  close(file_conn)
}

# # Example of how to use the function
# 
# test <- data.frame(Location = c("SiteA", "SiteB"), a = c(0,1))
# test_desc <- "This dataframe contains zeros and ones"
# test_column_desc <- c(
#   "Description of column 1",
#   "Description of column 2"
# )
# test_filename <- "test"
# # 
# # # Example without system time
# save_csv_and_metadata(
#   test,
#   test_desc,
#   test_column_desc,
#   test_filename,
#   time = TRUE
# )

## setting thresholds

# set some thresholds, including min and max survey years and maximum year added (to remove sites that have not been surveyed for the whole time period. these variables can be adjusted, but they will be applied uniformly to the 3 datasets.
                                                                            
minyear <- 1992

# EMBARGO (root toggle): the public site publishes nothing dated after embargo_date.
# It keeps pre-published data private during peer review. To LIFT the embargo once the
# paper is published, set embargo_date to a far-future date (for example
# as.Date("2100-12-31")) and re-render the whole site. maxyear derives from it so the
# benthic and temperature year filters stay in sync with the embargo.
embargo_date <- as.Date("2024-12-31")
maxyear <- as.integer(format(embargo_date, "%Y"))

maxyearadded <- 2007

# `minyear` = the earliest year of surveys to include

# `maxyear` = the latest year of surveys to include

# `maxyearadded` only include sites that were first surveyed no later than this year

# these thresholds mean that are only including surveys between `r minyear` and `r maxyear` for sites added `r maxyearadded` at the latest.

# site data

# This data contains information about the various monitoring sites.

# Central raw-data path (Phase D): configurable + self-diagnosing. Every page reads RRSdata via
# ../../../RRSdata (a sibling of the repo). Fail fast with a clear message if it is missing, instead
# of the cryptic mid-render file() error that a stale/absent path produces.
RRSdata_path <- Sys.getenv("RRSDATA_PATH", "../../../RRSdata")
if (!dir.exists(RRSdata_path))
  stop("RRSdata not found at '", RRSdata_path, "'. Set the RRSDATA_PATH env var, or place the ",
       "RRSdata repo as a sibling of RRS. Rendering runs from each page's directory, so the ",
       "default resolves three levels up.")

sitedat <-
  read.csv(
    file.path(RRSdata_path, "00_RRS_dataCatalogStatus/00_RRS_siteMaster_allSites_data.csv")
  )

```
